title: Hoya obreniformis
taxonomy: Hoya obreniformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Philippines. The species belongs to the genus Hoya, commonly known as the "wax plant" or "porcelain flower." Hoya obreniformis is a climber and has thick, waxy leaves. The plant is classified under the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Hoya obreniformis, also known as the Christmas waxy, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hoya obreniformis include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hoya obreniformis is often made into a decoction or infused in tea, which is used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other health issues.
